description:
You are an observability engineer who has been asked to create a very small “one-off” report that will later be used to tune a Grafana dashboard.

    1.  A raw application event log already exists at  
        /home/user/observability/logs/app_events.log  
        The file contains 60 single-line entries formatted exactly like this:  

            2023-08-01T10:00:01Z EVT_DATA_SYNC user=alice  
            2023-08-01T10:00:06Z EVT_LOGIN     user=bob  
            …  

        •  The first field is the timestamp (ISO-8601, no spaces).  
        •  The second field is the event-code (all caps, underscore-separated, no spaces).  
        •  Anything after the second field can be ignored.  

        There are exactly five different event-codes in the file:  
        EVT_DATA_SYNC, EVT_LOGIN, EVT_LOGOUT, EVT_ERROR and EVT_TIMEOUT.  

    2.  Your task is to produce a frequency-count report of how many times each distinct
        event-code appears in the file.  
        •  Use only classic *nix text-processing tools (e.g. sort, uniq, cut, awk, etc.).  
        •  For the purposes of this exercise you may assume the log file will never exceed a few MiB, so an all-in-memory, one-liner pipeline is fine.  

    3.  Put the finished report in  
        /home/user/observability/reports/event_code_frequency.log  
        (create the /home/user/observability/reports directory if it does not yet exist).

    4.  The report MUST follow **exactly** this plaintext format so that automated tests can verify it:  

        Line-1:  # EVENT CODE FREQUENCY REPORT  
        Line-2:  # Generated on YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ   ← use the current UTC time of generation  
        Line-3:  (blank)  
        From line-4 onward: one line per event code, sorted by count descending
        (numeric, largest to smallest).  
        Each of those lines must look like  

            <count><single-space><event_code>  

        Example (illustrative only, real numbers may differ):  

            20 EVT_DATA_SYNC  
            14 EVT_LOGIN  
            11 EVT_LOGOUT  
             9 EVT_ERROR  
             6 EVT_TIMEOUT  

        •  Counts must be right-aligned to a width of 2 (pad with a leading
           space if the count is a single digit).  
        •  There must be exactly one blank line (line-3) and no trailing blank
           line at the end of the file.  

    5.  When you are satisfied, display the completed report to the console so
        the user can eyeball it.

    Hints (do NOT print these commands, they are only conceptual):  
        – extract the 2nd whitespace-separated column,  
        – sort it,  
        – use uniq -c to count,  
        – sort -nr to order by frequency,  
        – use printf/awk to enforce the 2-char right-aligned count column and
          final formatting.
truth:

        Ground-truth file that is pre-created for the agent.
    -->
    <initial_file path="/home/user/observability/logs/app_events.log"><![CDATA[

    <!--
        Expected frequency counts that tests will check for.
    -->
    <expected_counts>
        <code value="EVT_DATA_SYNC" count="20"/>
        <code value="EVT_LOGIN"     count="14"/>
        <code value="EVT_LOGOUT"    count="11"/>
        <code value="EVT_ERROR"     count="9"/>
        <code value="EVT_TIMEOUT"   count="6"/>
    </expected_counts>

    <!--
        The test harness will allow for any current timestamp on lines 1-2 but
        will verify that:
          • the two header lines exist exactly as specified (aside from the
            actual timestamp),
          • line-3 is blank,
          • the five data lines exist, are in descending numerical order and
            match the counts specified above,
          • counts are right-aligned to width-2,
          • there is no trailing blank line.
    -->
